title: Home timeline: SQL join over follows, paginated, local+remote posts
description:
Compute home timeline on read via SQL — no fanout cache. UNION of: posts from local accounts followed by the authed user + remote_posts from remote accounts followed by the authed user. Sort by ID descending. Support max_id, since_id, min_id, limit params. Each row serialized as full Status object.

Agent workflow: Spawn a research subagent to plan the SQL query (with correct UNION and ordering across two tables with heterogeneous schemas). Spawn a testing subagent to write timeline correctness tests with known test data.

Test discipline:
- Insert 3 posts from followed accounts + 2 from unfollowed → only 3 appear
- Posts from unfollowed accounts don't appear even if public
- Paginate: fetch 2 at a time through 10 posts → receive all 10 in correct order
- Mixed local+remote posts → correct chronological ordering by snowflake ID
- Boost in timeline: appears as boost wrapper with reblog field set

Defensive programming:
- Query timeout: if home timeline query takes > 2s, log warning (indicative of missing index)
- Empty timeline: return [] with no Link header
- min_id vs since_id semantics differ: min_id returns posts after that ID (newest first within constraint); since_id returns posts after that ID sorted oldest first — test both
acceptance_criteria: Only followed accounts' posts appear; paginated correctly; local+remote posts correctly ordered; boosts appear as wrappers; query runs < 100ms on test dataset
